homepage.text_excerpt: Paul Davies MP – Member of Parliament for Colne Valley Facebook About Me News Speeches In Parliament In the Constituency Tackling Loneliness Literacy in Prisons Contact Me Office Standards Statement Privacy Policy PAUL DAVIES MP Colne Valley is my home. I'm proud of our community and I want it to be the best that can be. That's why I'm so honoured to stand up for you as our MP. ABOUT PAUL Prisons Literacy Project When we talk about reducing crime, the conversation often gravitates toward tougher sentences or more policing. Yet, the Prisons Literacy Project, spearheaded by MP Paul Davies and author Lee Child, offers a refreshingly pragmatic alternative: tackle reoffending by improving literacy. The logic is compelling. It costs £55,000 a year to keep someone in prison. If boosting literacy can cut reoffending by even 10%, the savings to taxpayers are enormous—not to mention the human benefit of fewer victims and more rehabilitated lives. This isn’t about being “soft on crime.” As Lee Child quipped, “Just look at my Jack Reacher books.” It’s about evidence-based solutions that work. If we truly want safer communities, we must invest in solutions that break cycles of crime. Literacy is not a luxury; it’s a lifeline. pages.meta_description: I moved to Holmfirth 14 years ago with my family and have made the Colne Valley constituency my home. headings: h2: About Me
pages.text_excerpt: About Me – Paul Davies MP Skip to content Facebook About Me News Speeches In Parliament In the Constituency Tackling Loneliness Literacy in Prisons Contact Me Office Standards Statement Privacy Policy About Me I moved to Holmfirth 14 years ago with my family and have made the Colne Valley constituency my home. In recent years, I have had the privilege of serving as a parish councillor and as a councillor for Holme Valley South. I have also had the honour of serving as the lead councillor for the environment and fighting the cost of living crisis, introducing affordable food hubs across the district, introducing warm welcome spaces in our libraries, and developing a climate change action plan. I grew up in the mining village of Caerau, Wales, and began my career as a coal face electrician. My upbringing taught my the values of hard work and solidarity, and I was lucky to attend Ruskin College, Oxford, on a trade union bursary and later Cardiff University before beginning a career in HR and operations management. From the mining village of Caerau to being an Operations Director for a Global IT company, a teacher in Linthwaite and a ward councillor for Holme Valley South, I have had a long and varied career which has taken me all over the world. I’m proud of our home and I want it to be the best it can be. That’s why I’m proud to be your Member of Parliament for Colne Valley. Parliamentary Groups Member of the Petitions Committee Chair of the All-Party Parliamentary Carbon Monoxide Group Co-chair of the All-Party Parliamentary on Occupational Health Member of the All-Party Parliamentary on Dairy Member of the All-Party Parliamentary on markets Member of the All-Party Parliamentary on HIV, AIDS and Sexual Health Member of the All-Party Parliamentary on Yorkshire and Lancashire Member of the Labour friends of the Coalfields Member of the All-Party Parliamentary on Less Survivable Cancers Member of the All-Party Parliamentary on Prostate Cancer Member of the All-Party Parliamentary on Prostate Cancer Member of the All-Party Parliamentary on Parkinson’s Privacy policy Loading Comments... headings: h2: Contact Me
pages.text_excerpt: Contact Me – Paul Davies MP Skip to content Facebook About Me News Speeches In Parliament In the Constituency Tackling Loneliness Literacy in Prisons Contact Me Office Standards Statement Privacy Policy Contact Me Need to get in touch with me? You can reach out to me through the following means: paul.davies.mp@parliament.uk House of Commons, London, SW1A 0AA 01484 598600 Whether it’s a concern that you have been treated unfairly, a local issue or to ask a policy question, your voice matters! There is a strict parliamentary protocol which dictates that MPs may only represent their constituents, so please check first by entering your postcode here . Please provide as many details as possible, including your name, address and full details of your issue If you are contacting me on behalf of someone else, I will only be able to help if you have permission from that person. This permission should be given in writing. I look forward to hearing from you. Email me Instagram Facebook Emergency Numbers Emergency Servic es : Immediate danger, for police fire services or medical emergencies call 999 For non emergencies call 101 For non-life-threatening emergencies, needing medical help or immediate medical advice call 111 Kirklees Council: Council housing repairs – out of hours council repairs 01484 414850 Pinnacle Housing out of hours repairs 0330 036 1517 Housing, homelessness, and financial hardship homelessness (in hours) 01484 221350 & homelessness (out of hours) 01484 414933 , benefits (local welfare provision) 01484 414782 council vulnerable tenant 01484 414886 Abuse and domestic violence child abuse and domestic violence (in hours) 01484 456848 , child abuse (out of hours) 01484 414933 Pennine Domestic Violence Group 0800 0527222 National Domestic Violence Helpline 0808 2000 247 ·Roads, weather and emergency events flooding (Kirklees Council hotlines) 01484 414700 (8am-6pm) & 01484 414888 (6pm-8am) flooding (Environment Agency hotline) 0345 988 1188 road emergencies 01484 414700 severe weather damage 01484 414888 noise 01484 414828 ·Social care adults social care (24hrs) 01484 414933 children social care (in hours) 01484 456848 children social care (emergency out of hours) 999 Utility : Northern Gas 0800 111 999 Northern Power Grid 0800 375 675 or 105 in the event of a powercut Yorkshire Water (in hours) report a problem online https://tell-us.yorkshirewater.com/ & Yorkshire Water (out of hours) 0345 1242 424 Privacy policy Loading Comments...
